evals.prompt: run a post-mortem on this: we had Claude implement the intake form submission feature. all the unit tests passed, Claude said it was done. but when i actually went to test it in the browser, clicking submit with an empty required field just silently did nothing — no error message, no validation, just nothing. the form wasn't validating at all. the tests were all mocking the validation function so they passed even though the real implementation was broken.
evals.expected_output: A complete COE document saved to docs/postmortems/ with a five-whys that reaches a systemic root cause (not 'we forgot to test'), lessons learned that call out the mock-over-test problem, and action items tagged with types like [TEST] and [CLAUDE.md] that are specific and concrete.
expectations: Creates a file in docs/postmortems/ with a date-prefixed name
Document contains a Five Whys section with at least 4 why iterations
Root cause identifies something systemic (e.g., mocked tests don't verify real behavior, no E2E coverage, no browser verification requirement)
At least one action item is tagged [TEST]
At least one action item is tagged [CLAUDE.md] or [HOOK]
Action items are specific (not vague like 'improve testing')
Document contains a Summary, Timeline, Resolution, Lessons Learned, and Action Items section

evals.prompt: /coe i had 4 PRs open and asked you to merge them all in sequence. after the merges, i noticed that the dark mode toggle feature was completely gone — it had been implemented in PR #2 but got clobbered when PR #4 merged because they both touched the same ThemeContext file. there was no conflict detected because PR #4 branched off master before PR #2 was merged, so git didn't see it as a conflict.
evals.expected_output: A COE document that correctly identifies the silent clobber as the failure mode, traces it to the rebase/merge order problem, and produces action items that would prevent or detect this (e.g., requiring rebase-before-merge, adding CI checks, or changing the merge workflow).
expectations: Creates a file in docs/postmortems/ with a date-prefixed name
Five Whys reaches the root cause around branch ordering / no rebase-before-merge requirement
Action items address the merge workflow specifically
At least one action item is tagged [PROCESS] or [CI]
Document correctly identifies this as a silent clobber (not a merge conflict)
Document contains all required sections: Summary, Timeline, Resolution, Five Whys, Lessons Learned, Action Items

evals.prompt: post-mortem: I asked claude to build the CSV export feature for the work items table. claude implemented it and said it was done. i verified it worked for small datasets. a week later we had 500+ work items and the export just hung indefinitely — the page froze, never downloaded anything. turns out claude used a synchronous loop to build the CSV string in memory and the browser just choked. there were no tests for large datasets and claude never mentioned any performance considerations.
evals.expected_output: A COE with a five-whys that reaches something about how Claude declares features done without performance testing or with-data verification, and action items that address both the test gap and the process gap around declaring features complete.
expectations: Creates a file in docs/postmortems/ with a date-prefixed name
Five Whys reaches a root cause about missing performance/scale testing or the definition-of-done not including scale verification
At least one action item is tagged [TEST] addressing scale/performance testing
At least one action item is tagged [CLAUDE.md] or [PROCESS] addressing when a feature can be declared done
Action items are specific enough to be actionable
